covered_report_window: Monday, June 22, 2026 after the U.S. market close, plus Tuesday, June 23, 2026 before the U.S. market open. The run occurred while the U.S. market was still open, so Tuesday after-close reporters are excluded.
market_context_summary: U.S. equities were modestly softer intraday, with QQQ weaker than SPY. Investors were watching U.S.-Iran peace-talk headlines, oil-price volatility, gold, Thursday PCE inflation data, and a light earnings calendar.

ranked_companies.timing: Tuesday, June 23, 2026 before U.S. market open; 10:00 AM ET call
ranked_companies.one_sentence_thesis: Carnival has the strongest upside skew in the window because demand, bookings, deposits, and yield evidence remain strong, but the stock has rallied into the print and fuel/geopolitical risk can quickly reverse a good headline quarter.
ranked_companies.market_expectation_summary: Company IR confirms a June 23 Q2 earnings call; public sources show consensus near $0.34-$0.35 EPS and about $6.69B revenue, with options sources indicating a mid-to-high single-digit implied move.
ranked_companies.upside_case_summary: Upside requires net yields above the roughly 2% Q2 guide, resilient onboard spend, strong bookings/deposits, and reassurance that fuel pressure is manageable.
ranked_companies.downside_case_summary: Downside comes from fuel cost sensitivity, no fuel hedging, weaker forward yield language, EPS guidance pressure, or a sell-the-news reaction after the June rally.
ranked_companies.options_or_positioning_summary: TipRanks listed CCL's weekly implied move near +/-7.68%; Investing.com cited a 5.6% move from Bloomberg options data. IEX bars show the stock rose from $23.89 on May 19 to about $30.43 intraday June 22, raising reversal risk.
ranked_companies.analyst_revision_summary: Public sources show a generally bullish analyst backdrop, but Simply Wall St noted FY2026 EPS estimates had fallen from $2.49 to $2.23 amid fuel concerns, which tempers the upside case.
ranked_companies.pass_agreement: Pass A +42 / reversal 45; Pass B +34 / reversal 50; Pass C +36 / reversal 46. All three leaned up, but each flagged fuel and priced-in risk.

ranked_companies.timing: Tuesday, June 23, 2026 before U.S. market open; results before 8:30 AM ET call
ranked_companies.one_sentence_thesis: Sunbelt leans up because Q3 showed sequential rental-revenue improvement, raised FY26 rental-growth midpoint, strong free cash flow and buyback support, but the stock needs constructive FY27 demand and capex commentary.
ranked_companies.market_expectation_summary: Company confirms a June 23 Q4/FY26 call; Q3 rental revenue grew 2.6%, FY26 rental revenue guidance was narrowed to 2%-3%, and free cash flow guidance was approximately $2B.
ranked_companies.upside_case_summary: Upside comes from stable or improving dollar utilization, continued mega-project demand, local non-residential stabilization, controlled capex, and buyback/free-cash-flow support.
ranked_companies.downside_case_summary: Downside comes from margin/cost pressure, FY27 slowing, higher capex with weaker FCF conversion, lower utilization, or a sell-the-news reaction after recent strength.
ranked_companies.options_or_positioning_summary: Reliable current implied-move data was not accessible. IEX bars show SUNB rose from $71.84 on May 11 to $83.39 intraday June 22, so positioning risk is present.
ranked_companies.analyst_revision_summary: Public analyst snapshots are mixed-to-positive, but average target information is clustered near current trading levels, limiting the upside signal.
ranked_companies.pass_agreement: Pass A +36 / reversal 38; Pass B +29 / reversal 40; Pass C +32 / reversal 39. All three leaned up with low disparity.

ranked_companies.timing: Tuesday, June 23, 2026 before U.S. market open; 12:00 PM ET webcast
ranked_companies.one_sentence_thesis: Korn Ferry mildly leans up because Q3 fee revenue grew across all solutions and Q4 guidance brackets consensus, but the market likely needs FY2027 client-demand confidence rather than just an in-line Q4.
ranked_companies.market_expectation_summary: Company confirms a before-open release; management guided Q4 FY26 fee revenue to $730-$750M and diluted EPS of $1.34-$1.40, while public calendars list EPS around $1.38-$1.39.
ranked_companies.upside_case_summary: Upside comes from fee revenue above the guide midpoint, resilient Executive Search and Consulting demand, stable adjusted EBITDA margin, and constructive FY2027 tone.
ranked_companies.downside_case_summary: Downside comes from softer client decision cycles, weak consulting/backlog consumption, compensation cost pressure, or a merely in-line quarter with cautious guidance.
ranked_companies.options_or_positioning_summary: TipRanks listed KFY's implied move near +/-9.24%; IEX bars show a pullback from early-June highs into the event, reducing sell-the-news risk but not creating a strong squeeze setup.
ranked_companies.analyst_revision_summary: Benzinga shows Truist maintained Buy with an $80 target on June 9; the signal is supportive but not decisive.
ranked_companies.pass_agreement: Pass A +30 / reversal 34; Pass B +22 / reversal 38; Pass C +26 / reversal 36. Two Lean Up and one high-Neutral; mild disagreement capped confidence.

qualified_names.why: Absolute score above 30, reversal risk below or equal to 40, low disparity, and adequate evidence quality.

excluded_names.reason: Score above 30, but reversal risk of 47 exceeds the allocation filter because fuel and priced-in demand risks are material.
Direction score of 26 is below the allocation threshold.

research_allocation_view.plain_statement: This is a research prioritization model, not financial advice. The hypothetical same-day research basket assigns 60% to SUNB and leaves 40% unallocated rather than forcing weaker ideas.

detailed_company_sections.consensus_summary: All three passes leaned up on demand/yield evidence, but all raised reversal risk because the stock rallied into earnings and fuel/geopolitical exposure remains central.
detailed_company_sections.pass_agreement: Consistent Lean Up with moderate caution.
detailed_company_sections.recommendation: Lean Up; excluded from allocation because reversal risk is above 40.
detailed_company_sections.one_sentence_thesis: If Q2 net yields and forward bookings confirm demand strength while fuel commentary is contained, CCL can rise, but the setup is vulnerable to a guidance or fuel-driven reversal.
detailed_company_sections.what_market_expects: EPS around $0.34-$0.35, revenue around $6.69B, focus on net yield, onboard spend, deposits, fuel cost assumptions, and full-year EPS/EBITDA guidance.
detailed_company_sections.what_could_make_stock_go_up: Net yields clear the Q2 bar, bookings/deposits stay strong, onboard revenue is resilient, and management reassures on fuel and PROPEL targets.
detailed_company_sections.what_could_make_stock_go_down: Fuel assumptions worsen, FY EPS guide pressure returns, net-yield language softens, or the stock fades because the demand beat was already priced in.
detailed_company_sections.reversal_risk_assessment: Moderate due to recent price momentum and unhedged fuel sensitivity.
detailed_company_sections.reddit_social_consumer_sentiment_read: Retail/social attention is visible but noisy; consumer-demand evidence comes mainly from bookings/deposit disclosures.
detailed_company_sections.options_positioning_read: Options sources show a mid-to-high single-digit expected move.
detailed_company_sections.analyst_revision_read: Supportive analyst tone is offset by FY2026 EPS estimate cuts tied to fuel risk.
detailed_company_sections.base_case: Solid Q2 and stable guide produce a modest positive reaction.
detailed_company_sections.bull_case: Net yields, bookings, and onboard spend beat while fuel assumptions stabilize.
detailed_company_sections.bear_case: Fuel and FY guidance dominate despite headline strength.
detailed_company_sections.final_directional_judgment: Lean Up, score 37, reversal risk 47, Medium confidence.
detailed_company_sections.allocation_rationale: Excluded because reversal risk is above 40.
detailed_company_sections.disparity_effect: Low-to-moderate disparity supported direction, but not allocation.

detailed_company_sections.consensus_summary: All three passes leaned up and centered rental-growth durability and FCF/capex tone.
detailed_company_sections.pass_agreement: Tight agreement with modest score spread.
detailed_company_sections.one_sentence_thesis: SUNB can react positively if FY27 demand, dollar utilization, and free-cash-flow commentary confirm that Q3's sequential improvement was durable.
detailed_company_sections.what_market_expects: A pre-open Q4/FY26 release focused on whether Q3's 2%-3% FY26 rental-revenue outlook carries into FY27.
detailed_company_sections.what_could_make_stock_go_up: Rental revenue above plan, stronger utilization, Specialty growth, mega-project wins, controlled capex, and FCF/buyback support.
detailed_company_sections.what_could_make_stock_go_down: Softer FY27 demand, cost inflation, higher capex without FCF upside, lower dollar utilization, or sell-the-news behavior.
detailed_company_sections.reversal_risk_assessment: Low-to-moderate; company data supports the setup but the stock has rerated.
detailed_company_sections.reddit_social_consumer_sentiment_read: Sparse direct Reddit discussion; no strong retail crowding signal.
detailed_company_sections.options_positioning_read: Reliable implied-move data not accessible; recent price appreciation is the main positioning risk.
detailed_company_sections.analyst_revision_read: Mixed-positive analyst backdrop, not enough for high confidence.
detailed_company_sections.base_case: Constructive Q4 with steady FY27 commentary yields a modest positive reaction.
detailed_company_sections.bull_case: Stronger FY27 rental growth and FCF conversion with disciplined capex.
detailed_company_sections.bear_case: Cost pressure, utilization, or capex-heavy growth drives a fade.
detailed_company_sections.final_directional_judgment: Lean Up, score 32, reversal risk 39, Medium confidence.
detailed_company_sections.allocation_rationale: Included at 60% because it cleared score, reversal-risk, disparity, and evidence filters.

detailed_company_sections.consensus_summary: The passes agreed on a mild positive setup but differed on whether the edge cleared the threshold.
detailed_company_sections.pass_agreement: Two Lean Up and one high-Neutral.
detailed_company_sections.recommendation: Lean Up, but not allocation-priority quality.
detailed_company_sections.one_sentence_thesis: KFY has a mild upside path if Q4 fee revenue and FY27 demand commentary exceed a consensus bar already near the guide midpoint.
detailed_company_sections.what_market_expects: Q4 EPS around $1.38-$1.39 and fee revenue near the $730-$750M guide.
detailed_company_sections.what_could_make_stock_go_up: Better fee revenue, Executive Search and Professional Search strength, stable margin, and upbeat FY27 demand commentary.
detailed_company_sections.what_could_make_stock_go_down: Cautious FY27 demand, slowing consulting activity, margin pressure, or merely in-line results.
detailed_company_sections.reversal_risk_assessment: Low-to-moderate; pullback helps, but cautious forward tone can overwhelm Q4.
detailed_company_sections.options_positioning_read: TipRanks lists about a 9.24% implied move; edge does not clearly exceed the event price.
detailed_company_sections.analyst_revision_read: Supportive but not decisive; Truist maintained Buy with $80 target.
detailed_company_sections.base_case: Near-guide Q4 and stable FY27 tone lead to a muted-to-slightly-positive reaction.
detailed_company_sections.bull_case: Revenue clears high end and demand tone improves.
detailed_company_sections.bear_case: Forward commentary points to slower starts or weaker backlog consumption.
detailed_company_sections.final_directional_judgment: Lean Up, score 26, reversal risk 36, Medium confidence.
detailed_company_sections.allocation_rationale: Excluded because score is below threshold.
detailed_company_sections.disparity_effect: Low disparity helped confidence, but one high-Neutral pass pulled it closer to Neutral.

disclaimer_text: This is research, not financial advice. Earnings reactions are highly uncertain and can be driven by market positioning, guidance, macro conditions, and management commentary rather than reported results alone.
